content: I've been thinking about getting a pet, but I'm not sure what would work with my schedule. I work as a paramedic in the city and my shifts are pretty unpredictable.
That's a great question! With irregular hours, you'd want something relatively independent. Have you considered cats, or maybe a low-maintenance aquarium setup?
I actually already have a freshwater aquarium with some tetras and a betta. I find watching them really relaxing after a tough shift. But I'd love something more interactive too.
An aquarium sounds perfect for unwinding. For something more interactive that can handle alone time, have you thought about an older cat from a shelter? They're usually more settled than kittens.
That's a good idea. I've been learning Mandarin lately too, since I'm planning to move to Brazil next year. I wonder if I'll have time for both language practice and pet care.

Brazil is an exciting move! While you're still here, an older cat could be a great companion. They don't need constant attention - just regular feeding, a clean litter box, and some playtime when you're home.
True. I've been getting into pottery recently too, which I could do while the cat lounges nearby. Do you think a 10-year-old cat would be too old to adjust to a new home?

Not at all! Older cats often appreciate a calm, loving home and can form strong bonds. Plus, they're usually already litter-trained and have established personalities, so you'll know what you're getting.
That makes sense. I'll check out the shelter this weekend. Maybe I can find one that matches my energy level - calm but still playful sometimes.
That sounds perfect! Let me know how it goes. And don't forget to get some toys - even older cats enjoy a good wand toy or puzzle feeder to keep them stimulated when you're at work.
Will do! Thanks for the advice. I'm excited to give an older cat a good home before my big move abroad.
